steps.title: Identify the structure around the blank
steps.content: To the left of the blank is the incomplete noun phrase “Generations of mystery and horror.” To the right is the main verb “have been influenced.” The blank must contain a noun to complete the subject.

steps.title: Apply the subject-verb punctuation rule
steps.content:
Standard English requires: no punctuation between a complete subject and its main verb.

Option A provides “writers” with no additional punctuation, correctly maintaining the direct connection between subject and verb.

Options B, C, and D insert punctuation (comma, em dash, semicolon) between subject and verb, each violating this rule.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
